content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 4. –ü–æ—á–µ–º—É `Note` –Ω–µ –∑–∞–º–µ–Ω—è–µ—Ç `Claim` .. 5. Note maturity
Document lines: 540-583

## 4. –ü–æ—á–µ–º—É `Note` –Ω–µ –∑–∞–º–µ–Ω—è–µ—Ç `Claim`

–ü–æ—Ç–æ–º—É —á—Ç–æ —É –Ω–∏—Ö —Ä–∞–∑–Ω–∞—è —Ä–∞–±–æ—Ç–∞.

### `Claim`
–ù—É–∂–µ–Ω –¥–ª—è:
- —Ç–æ—á–Ω—ã—Ö factual answers
- reconciliation —á–µ—Ä–µ–∑ predicate rules
- profile updates
- time-aware status answers

### `Note`
–ù—É–∂–µ–Ω –¥–ª—è:
- richer retrieval
- decision history
- rationale
- partial or evolving meaning
- later consolidation

–ï—Å–ª–∏ —Ö—Ä–∞–Ω–∏—Ç—å –≤—Å–µ –∫–∞–∫ `Claim`, –ø–æ–ª—É—á–∏—Ç—Å—è –ª–æ–∂–Ω–∞—è —Ç–æ—á–Ω–æ—Å—Ç—å.
–ï—Å–ª–∏ —Ö—Ä–∞–Ω–∏—Ç—å –≤—Å–µ –∫–∞–∫ `Note`, –ø–æ–ª—É—á–∏—Ç—Å—è —Å–ª–∏—à–∫–æ–º –º—è–≥–∫–∞—è –ø–∞–º—è—Ç—å.

## 5. Note maturity

`Note` –Ω–µ–ª—å–∑—è –æ—Å—Ç–∞–≤–ª—è—Ç—å –±–µ–∑ –º–æ–¥–µ–ª–∏ –∑—Ä–µ–ª–æ—Å—Ç–∏.

–Ý–µ–∫–æ–º–µ–Ω–¥—É–µ–º—ã–π –ø–∞—Ç—Ç–µ—Ä–Ω:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`
- `consolidated`

–ß—Ç–æ –º–æ–∂–µ—Ç –ø–æ–≤—ã—à–∞—Ç—å maturity:
- note –∏—Å–ø–æ–ª—å–∑–æ–≤–∞–ª—Å—è –≤ retrieval
- note –ø–æ–¥–¥–µ—Ä–∂–∞–Ω –Ω–µ—Å–∫–æ–ª—å–∫–∏–º–∏ sources
- note –ø–µ—Ä–µ–∂–∏–ª –≤—Ä–µ–º—è –±–µ–∑ supersede
- note –ø–æ–∫—Ä—ã–≤–∞–µ—Ç recurring rationale questions

–ß—Ç–æ –º–æ–∂–µ—Ç –ø–æ–Ω–∏–∂–∞—Ç—å / –æ–±–Ω—É–ª—è—Ç—å:
- contradiction
- supersede
- scope drift
- long-term disuse

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DIRECTION title=Claim vs Note separation by function and trade-off summary=The model keeps `Claim` and `Note` as distinct memory types: `Claim` is for precise factual answers, predicate-based reconciliation, profile updates, and time-aware status; `Note` is for richer retrieval, decision history, rationale, evolving meaning, and later consolidation. Collapsing everything into one type causes either false precision (`Claim`-only) or overly soft memory (`Note`-only). scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 4. –ü–æ—á–µ–º—É `Note` –Ω–µ –∑–∞–º–µ–Ω—è–µ—Ç `Claim`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1> keywords=Claim,Note,reconciliation,rationale,false precision tags=data_model,memory_types,tradeoff evidence=–ï—Å–ª–∏ —Ö—Ä–∞–Ω–∏—Ç—å –≤—Å–µ –∫–∞–∫ `Claim`, –ø–æ–ª—É—á–∏—Ç—Å—è –ª–æ–∂–Ω–∞—è —Ç–æ—á–Ω–æ—Å—Ç—å. –ï—Å–ª–∏ —Ö—Ä–∞–Ω–∏—Ç—å –≤—Å–µ –∫–∞–∫ `Note`, –ø–æ–ª—É—á–∏—Ç—Å—è —Å–ª–∏—à–∫–æ–º –º—è–≥–∫–∞—è –ø–∞–º—è—Ç—å.
[1] type=PLAN title=Note maturity lifecycle and promotion/demotion signals summary=`Note` should follow a maturity model with stages `fresh` ‚Üí `stabilizing` ‚Üí `mature` ‚Üí `consolidated`. Maturity can increase via retrieval usage, multi-source support, surviving time without supersede, and answering recurring rationale questions; it can decrease/reset due to contradiction, supersede, scope drift, or long-term disuse. scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 5. Note maturity' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<2>,entity:<3> keywords=note maturity,fresh,stabilizing,mature,consolidated tags=lifecycle,quality,governance evidence=–Ý–µ–∫–æ–º–µ–Ω–¥—É–µ–º—ã–π –ø–∞—Ç—Ç–µ—Ä–Ω: - `fresh` - `stabilizing` - `mature` - `consolidated`

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Note` is a standalone semantic fragment between source and claim summary=In this model, `Note` captures retrieval-useful but not-yet-hardened knowledge (e.g., decision rationale, evolving plans, hypotheses, lessons, doc digests, context). A note must be self-contained, add value beyond source text, keep `candidate_claims_json` as hints only, and may remain long-lived without becoming a claim. scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 3. –ì–ª–∞–≤–Ω—ã–µ —Å—É—â–Ω–æ—Å—Ç–∏ / 3.4 `Note`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Note,semantic fragment,candidate_claims_json,retrieval_state,invariants tags=data_model,note_semantics,memory_lifecycle useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Entity` canonicalization model with aliases and reversible merges summary=`Entity` is the canonical memory node used to unify mentions and anchor claims/tasks/notes/episodes, with `entity_aliases` tracked in a separate table; merges must preserve history, aliases must resolve to the current canonical entity, and incorrect merges must be reversible via audit trail. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. –ì–ª–∞–≤–Ω—ã–µ —Å—É—â–Ω–æ—Å—Ç–∏ / 3.1 `Source` .. 3.2 `Entity`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Entity,entity_aliases,canonical entity,merge,audit trail tags=data_model,entity_resolution,aliases,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 data model layering and cross-cutting concerns summary=The `v3` data model is intentionally split into layers (`Source`, `Entity`/`Claim`, `Note`, `Profile`/`Task`/`Episode`, `MemoryRun`) to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability; it also defines two cross-cutting concerns: `scope` and `retrieval lifecycle`. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 1. –û–±—â–∞—è –∏–¥–µ—è' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=v3 data model,layering,typed memory,retrieval lifecycle tags=data_model,architecture,memory_layers useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=PredicateCatalog defines reconciliation semantics per predicate summary=`PredicateCatalog` is a configuration layer (not a memory item) used by reconciliation to encode per-predicate behavior: object kind, cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict resolution, and projection flags (`profile_sync`, `task_sync`). This allows different predicates to replace, coexist, or split by range according to policy. scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 3. –ì–ª–∞–≤–Ω—ã–µ —Å—É—â–Ω–æ—Å—Ç–∏ / 3.3 `PredicateCatalog`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=PredicateCatalog,reconciliation,cardinality,temporal_policy,conflict_policy tags=data_model,predicate_config,reconciliation useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Profile` is a compact, rebuildable projection layer (not source of truth) summary=Section `3.6 Profile` defines `Profile` as a short always-in-context projection derived from explicit instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context; it should prioritize explicit facts, avoid controversial facts, and remain rebuildable/compact. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. –ì–ª–∞–≤–Ω—ã–µ —Å—É—â–Ω–æ—Å—Ç–∏ / 3.6 `Profile`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Profile,projection layer,always-in-context,rebuildable tags=data_model,profile,projection,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Scope fields and truth-vs-retrieval axis separation in v3 summary=For most memory items except raw `Source`, `v3` recommends dual scope fields (`scope_text` for human explainability and `scope_json` for machine retrieval/reconciliation). It also mandates separating `truth status` (knowledge validity) from `retrieval_state` (search participation/lifecycle such as `active`, `warm`, `cold`, `archived`, `deleted`), with usage fields (`use_count`, `last_used_at`, `la...[truncated 61 chars] scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 2. –û–±—â–∏–µ cross-cutting –ø–æ–ª—è' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=scope_text,scope_json,truth status,retrieval_state,usage fields tags=schema_guideline,cross_cutting,retrieval useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DECISION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`MemoryRun` invariants: durable run logs and idempotency via `input_hash` summary=The `MemoryRun` section defines two key invariants: run logs must not disappear silently, and `input_hash` should be used to support idempotency and retry safety. scope=Applies to document section '02. Data Model / 3. –ì–ª–∞–≤–Ω—ã–µ —Å—É—â–Ω–æ—Å—Ç–∏ / 3.9 `MemoryRun`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=MemoryRun invariants,run log retention,input_hash,idempotency,retry safety tags=invariant,durability,idempotency,memoryrun useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"operations":[{"action":"insert","candidate_index":0,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.89,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>"},{"link_type":"related","link_weight":0.72,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<3>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"New distinct guidance clarifying complementary roles of Claim vs Note and explicit trade-off; not duplicated in active notes.","target_note_id":null},{"action":"insert","candidate_index":1,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.83,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"Introduces explicit Note maturity lifecycle stages and promotion/demotion signals, which are not yet captured as a standalone reusable rule.","target_note_id":null}]}
